preview_text:
The model favors Argentina at 74%, with Algeria given only a 5% chance of victory. The underdog faces a steep climb, though the model does assign a substantial 21% probability to a draw, suggesting the match retains competitive uncertainty despite the gap in win likelihood.

The spread indicates Argentina enters as clear favorite, but the 21-point margin between win and loss probabilities is not overwhelming. A draw remains plausible and represents the second-most-likely outcome. The match is unlikely to be one-sided in execution, even if the model leans decisively toward an Argentina result.

The model and sportsbooks diverge on Algeria's prospects. Books and Polymarket both peg Algeria at roughly 11%, about 6 percentage points higher than the model's 5%. The model and market align closely on Argentina (within 6pp) and the draw (within 1pp). The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is more accurate; the divergence may reflect different methodologies or market-specific factors not captured in the statistical forecast.
